site.title: Mongolstay — Submit in 4 minutes. Pay. Done.
site.description: Three visa filings, one click each. J-1 → F-1 and B-1/B-2 → F-1 changes of status, $1,000 flat. Asylum applications, $3,000 flat. Licensed immigration attorneys included.

hero.sub: Two visa filings, one click each. Upload documents, pay a flat fee, book your in-person appointment. A licensed immigration attorney reviews everything before it touches USCIS.

j1f1.body: Stay in the U.S. as a student without leaving. We handle the I-539, the home-residency analysis, and the supporting legal brief.

b1b2f1.body: Visiting on a tourist or business visa and want to stay as a student? We file the I-539 and the supporting brief showing the change of intent.

asylum.body: If you fear returning to your country, we'll prepare your asylum application and stand with you through the interview. One year filing deadline applies.

how.sub: You spend four minutes online. We spend two weeks on the legal work. Then you walk out of our office with a filing — not homework.

01.p: Four quick questions. We tell you instantly whether to file and which form.

02.p: Drag & drop your passport, I-94, I-20, financials. Missing one? We collect it later.

03.p: Flat fee, attorney review included. Refunded if our attorneys decline your case.

04.p: Pick a 30-minute Zoom slot or request a callback. Walk away with your filing in motion.

wins.sub: Real USCIS approval notices from Mongolstay clients. We blur the personal details — the seal, the receipt number, and the word that matters all stay legible.

team.sub: Every Mongolstay filing is reviewed and signed by a licensed immigration attorney. The software handles the typing. We handle the law.

footer.address: 388 Market Street, Suite 1300 · San Francisco, CA 94111
footer.fine: © Mongolstay PLLC. Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

eligibility.h: A few questions about your situation.
eligibility.s: These determine your filing strategy. Nothing you say here is filed with USCIS.

documents.s: PDF, JPG, or PNG. Drop in whatever you can find — we collect the rest after we start your case.

schedule.s: Book a video appointment or request a callback. We'll confirm your contact info too.

payment.s: Cash today, card or Zelle once we send the link. Refunded if our attorneys decline your case after review.

elig.verdictS_j1f1: Based on your answers, we'll prepare an I-539 change of status petition. A licensed attorney will review before filing.
elig.verdictS_b1b2f1: Based on your answers, we'll prepare an I-539 change of status petition with a supporting brief showing your change of intent. A licensed attorney will review before filing.
elig.verdictS_asylum: Based on your answers, we'll prepare an I-589 asylum application. A licensed attorney will review before filing.
elig.ineligibleH: This filing won't work for you yet.
elig.ineligibleS: Based on what you've told us, USCIS will deny a change-of-status petition. Here's why:
elig.ineligibleNote: If your situation has details we haven't captured here,

reasons.notInUS: You must be physically inside the U.S. to file a change of status. From abroad, you'll apply for the visa at a U.S. consulate instead.
reasons.j1Expired: Your J-1 status has expired. USCIS won't approve a change of status once you're already out of status.
reasons.dsExpired: Your DS-2019 has already expired. USCIS denies I-539 petitions when the underlying program has lapsed.
reasons.waiverNeeded: The 2-year home residency requirement (212(e)) blocks change of status. You need a waiver first, or to return home for two years before changing status.
reasons.i94Expired: Your I-94 has already expired — you're out of authorized stay. Change of status requires a valid I-94 on the day USCIS receives your I-539.
reasons.preconceivedIntent: Telling USCIS you intended to study when you entered on B-1/B-2 typically results in a denial for misrepresentation. We'd need a different strategy.

inUS.q: Are you currently inside the United States?
inUS.hint: Change of status only works while you're physically in the U.S. If you're abroad, you'll need to apply for an F-1 visa at a U.S. consulate instead.

currentJ1.q: Are you currently in valid J-1 status?
currentJ1.hint: Valid means your DS-2019 hasn't expired and you haven't violated program terms. Check the end date on your DS-2019 — not your visa stamp.

dsExpiry.hint: Your DS-2019 is the program form your J-1 sponsor issued you. The expiration is in box #3. We need to file before it expires (or use the 30-day grace period).

waiver.q: Are you subject to the 2-year home residency requirement (212(e))?
waiver.hint: Check your J-1 visa stamp and DS-2019 — they will say either "Bearer is subject to 212(e)" or "Bearer is not subject to 212(e)." If it says NOT subject, the 2-year rule does not block changing to F-1.
waiver.note: (Mongolians who arrived on Summer Work & Travel are typically not subject to the 2-year rule — your DS-2019 will say so explicitly.)

i20.q: Do you have an I-20 from a SEVP-approved school?
i20.hint: The I-20 is issued by your future U.S. school after you're admitted and they've reviewed your financial documents.
i20.note: (If you don't have one yet, we'll help you obtain it before filing — same flat fee.)

sevisPaid.q: Have you already paid the SEVIS I-901 fee ($350)?
sevisPaid.hint: SEVIS I-901 is the $350 fee paid to the U.S. Student and Exchange Visitor Program. It must be paid before USCIS approves your F-1 status.
sevisPaid.note: (If you haven't paid yet, we'll handle it for you before filing — same flat fee.)

funds.q: Can you show proof of funds for at least one year?
funds.hint: Bank statements, sponsor letters, or an I-134 (Affidavit of Support) all count. The amount must cover one academic year of tuition + living costs as listed on your I-20.

inUS.q: Are you currently inside the United States?
inUS.hint: Change of status only works while you're physically in the U.S. If you're abroad, you'll need to apply for an F-1 visa at a U.S. consulate instead.

currentBStatus.q: Did you enter the U.S. on a B-1 or B-2 visa?
currentBStatus.hint: Most visitors enter on a B-2 (tourist). B-1 is for short business trips. Check your visa stamp and I-94 — both will show the class.
currentBStatus.note: (Good news: B-2 → F-1 has no 2-year home residency requirement. Unlike J-1, the 212(e) rule doesn't apply to visitors.)

bExpiry.q: When does your authorized stay (I-94 expiration) end?
bExpiry.hint: Your I-94 sets how long you can legally stay — not the visa stamp. Download your latest I-94 from i94.cbp.dhs.gov to see the exact date. We must file the I-539 before it expires.

intent.q: When you entered the U.S., did you intend to study?
intent.hint: USCIS denies B → F-1 changes when the visitor entered with preconceived intent to study (it's called "misrepresentation"). Most safe filings are made 60+ days after entry. Be honest — your attorney will work with whatever your real situation is.

i20.q: Do you have an I-20 from a SEVP-approved school?
i20.hint: The I-20 is issued by your future U.S. school after admission.
i20.note: (If you don't have one yet, we'll help you obtain it before filing — same flat fee.)

sevisPaid.q: Have you already paid the SEVIS I-901 fee ($350)?
sevisPaid.hint: SEVIS I-901 is the $350 fee paid to the U.S. Student and Exchange Visitor Program. It must be paid before USCIS approves your F-1 status.
sevisPaid.note: (If you haven't paid yet, we'll handle it for you before filing — same flat fee.)

funds.q: Can you show proof of funds for at least one year?
funds.hint: Bank statements, sponsor letters, or an I-134 (Affidavit of Support) all count. The amount must cover one academic year of tuition + living costs as listed on your I-20.

pay.afterBody: We start your case the same day. If we need additional documents, your attorney will message you directly. Track every status change, message, and document upload from your dashboard.
pay.afterStep1: We open your file and assign your attorney.
pay.afterStep2: Any missing document? Your attorney messages you with what's needed.
pay.afterStep3: Track everything — status, attorney messages, uploaded files — from your dashboard.

card.label: Card
card.body: Card payment is coming online soon. For now please choose cash (in-person) — once you confirm, we'll send a secure card link by email if you'd prefer to pay that way.

zelle.label: Zelle
zelle.body: Confirm with the reference below and we'll email you the Zelle handle to send ${total} to. We'll match your transfer to this filing automatically.
zelle.recipientHidden: Sent by email after you confirm
zelle.foot: We'll mark your filing paid once we receive the transfer (usually within minutes).

cash.label: Cash (today)
cash.body: Bring ${total} in cash or a cashier's check to our office today. We'll print your receipt, open your file, and start your case while you're here.
cash.foot: Cash is the fastest way to start right now — we'll have your file open the moment you walk in.

invoice.title: Invoice
invoice.attorneyIncl: Attorney review and in-person appointment included
invoice.zelleNote: We'll email you the Zelle handle. Send the amount with memo "{memo}" — we'll mark this paid on receipt.
invoice.cashNote: Bring this invoice and reference {reference} when you come in.
invoice.foot: Mongolstay PLLC. Attorney advertising. Refundable if attorneys decline the case after review.
